title: cymopterus glomeratus var. leibergii
intro: Cymopterus glomeratus var. leibergii, commonly known as Leiberg's spring-parsley or mountain spring-parsley, is a perennial plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, and is used to treat a variety of health issues, including colds, coughs, and respiratory infections. The health benefits of Cymopterus glomeratus var. leibergii include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and combat illness.
